key_points: The foundry landscape is experiencing a power shift from mobile-driven (Apple) to AI-driven (NVIDIA) demand.
Apple's silicon empire was built via deep vertical integration, 8,000+ engineers, and 15 design centers, making it highly defensible.
Alternative foundries like Intel 18A and Samsung are emerging as viable options, raising strategic questions for the future.

paragraphs.source: In 2013, TSMC made a $10 billion bet on a single customer. Morris Chang committed to building 20nm capacity with uncertain economics on the promise that Apple would fill those fabs. “I bet the company, but I didn’t think I would lose,” Chang later said. He was right. Apple’s A8 chip launched in 2014, and TSMC never looked back.

inline_elements.text: I bet the company, but I didn’t think I would lose

paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: In 2013, TSMC made a $10 billion bet on a single customer. Morris Chang committed to building 20nm capacity with uncertain economics on the promise that Apple would fill those fabs. “*I bet the company, but I didn’t think I would lose*,” Chang later said. He was right. Apple’s A8 chip launched in 2014, and TSMC never looked back.

Morris Chang committed to building 20nm capacity with uncertain economics on the promise that Apple would fill those fabs. “I bet the company, but I didn’t think I would lose,” Chang later said. He was right. Apple’s A8 chip launched in 2014, and TSMC never looked back.

parent_inline_elements.text: I bet the company, but I didn’t think I would lose

pro.text: 2013 年，台积电（TSMC）将 100 亿美元押注于单一客户。张忠谋在经济效益尚不明朗的情况下，仅凭苹果会填满这些晶圆厂（fab）产能的承诺，便拍板建设 20 纳米产能。“我押上了整个公司，但我没想过会输，”张忠谋后来回忆道。他的确赢了。苹果的 A8 芯片于 2014 年发布，台积电自此一路高歌猛进。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s annual spend at TSMC grew from $2B in 2014 to $24B in 2025. That is 12x in 12 years. Apple went from 9% of TSMC revenue to 25% at its peak and settled to 20% in 2025. More striking is Apple’s dominance at node launches: consistently >50% since 20nm and in some cases near 100%. Apple effectively funded the yield learning curve for every major node transition since 20nm.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple’s annual spend at TSMC grew from $2B in 2014 to $24B in 2025. That is 12x in 12 years. Apple went from 9% of TSMC revenue to 25% at its peak and settled to 20% in 2025. More striking is Apple’s dominance at node launches: consistently >50% since 20nm and in some cases near 100%. Apple effectively funded the yield learning curve for every major node transition since 20nm.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple’s annual spend at TSMC grew from $2B in 2014 to $24B in 2025. That is 12x in 12 years. Apple went from 9% of TSMC revenue to 25% at its peak and settled to 20% in 2025. More striking is Apple’s dominance at node launches: consistently >50% since 20nm and in some cases near 100%. Apple effectively funded the yield learning curve for every major node transition since 20nm.

pro.text: 苹果在台积电的年度支出从 2014 年的 20 亿美元增长到 2025 年的 240 亿美元。12 年间实现了 12 倍的跃升。苹果占台积电营收的比例从 9% 攀升至巅峰期的 25%，并在 2025 年稳定在 20%。更惊人的是苹果在新制程节点发布初期的统治力：自 20 纳米以来，其份额始终保持在 50% 以上，部分情况下甚至接近 100%。可以说，自 20 纳米以来的每一次重大制程节点迭代，其良率学习曲线的成本实际上都由苹果买了单。

paragraphs.source: The foundry model is dominant today. IDMs cannot support process development and fab capex with what is effectively a single customer. But even foundries need a “first and best” customer with large demand and deep pockets to fund their continued advance. Apple has been that customer for the last decade at TSMC. The juggernaut partnership has propelled both companies to new heights, put competitors in the dust, and fueled the chipmaking industry.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The foundry model is dominant today. IDMs cannot support process development and fab capex with what is effectively a single customer. But even foundries need a “first and best” customer with large demand and deep pockets to fund their continued advance. Apple has been that customer for the last decade at TSMC. The juggernaut partnership has propelled both companies to new heights, put competitors in the dust, and fueled the chipmaking industry.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The foundry model is dominant today. IDMs cannot support process development and fab capex with what is effectively a single customer. But even foundries need a “first and best” customer with large demand and deep pockets to fund their continued advance. Apple has been that customer for the last decade at TSMC. The juggernaut partnership has propelled both companies to new heights, put competitors in the dust, and fueled the chipmaking industry.

pro.text: 如今，晶圆代工模式占据主导地位。IDM 仅靠实质上的单一客户，根本无法支撑制程研发和晶圆厂资本支出（capex）。但即便是晶圆代工厂，也需要一个需求庞大且财力雄厚的“首发且最优”客户，来为其持续的技术演进提供资金。过去十年间，苹果在台积电扮演的正是这一角色。这对巨头联盟将两家公司双双推向新高度，将竞争对手远远甩在身后，并为整个芯片制造行业注入了强劲动力。

paragraphs.source: This report traces the Apple-TSMC relationship from Intel’s 2010 rejection through five distinct phases, examining how Apple built its chip empire through acquisitions and 8,000+ engineers across 15 design centers. We analyze why competitors have failed to replicate this vertical integration, map Apple’s manufacturing footprint across Fab 18 and advanced packaging facilities, and assess the strategic questions facing both companies as Intel 18A and Samsung re-engagement become viable alternatives. We’ll also discuss the next phase of the relationship. It’s not just Apple at the leading edge anymore. All of this is quantified with specifics from our proprietary Foundry Industry Model and Apple Wafer Demand Model.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: This report traces the Apple-TSMC relationship from Intel’s 2010 rejection through five distinct phases, examining how Apple built its chip empire through acquisitions and 8,000+ engineers across 15 design centers. We analyze why competitors have failed to replicate this vertical integration, map Apple’s manufacturing footprint across Fab 18 and advanced packaging facilities, and assess the strategic questions facing both companies as Intel 18A and Samsung re-engagement become viable alternatives. We’ll also discuss the next phase of the relationship. It’s not just Apple at the leading edge anymore. All of this is quantified with specifics from our proprietary [Foundry Industry Model](https://semianalysis.com/foundry-industry-model/) and [Apple Wafer Demand Model](https://semianalysis.com/institutional/apple-tsmc-foundry-model-webinar-recap-key-takeaways/).
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: This report traces the Apple-TSMC relationship from Intel’s 2010 rejection through five distinct phases, examining how Apple built its chip empire through acquisitions and 8,000+ engineers across 15 design centers. We analyze why competitors have failed to replicate this vertical integration, map Apple’s manufacturing footprint across Fab 18 and advanced packaging facilities, and assess the strategic questions facing both companies as Intel 18A and Samsung re-engagement become viable alternatives. We’ll also discuss the next phase of the relationship. It’s not just Apple at the leading edge anymore. All of this is quantified with specifics from our proprietary Foundry Industry Model and Apple Wafer Demand Model.

pro.text: 本报告追溯了苹果与台积电的合作关系。我们从 2010 年苹果遭英特尔（Intel）拒单开始，梳理了双方历经的五个截然不同的阶段，并探讨苹果如何通过多笔收购以及分布在 15 个设计中心的 8000 多名工程师，构筑起庞大的芯片帝国。我们将分析竞争对手为何始终无法复制这种垂直整合，梳理苹果在 Fab 18 及先进封装设施中的制造版图，并评估随着英特尔18A 与三星（Samsung）重新入局成为可行的替代方案，双方所面临的战略抉择。我们还将探讨双方合作关系的下一阶段。先进制程领域的玩家，如今已不再只有苹果。上述所有观点均由我们独家晶圆代工行业模型与苹果晶圆需求模型的具体数据提供量化支撑。
pro.tokenized_text: 本报告追溯了苹果与台积电的合作关系。我们从 2010 年苹果遭英特尔（Intel）拒单开始，梳理了双方历经的五个截然不同的阶段，并探讨苹果如何通过多笔收购以及分布在 15 个设计中心的 8000 多名工程师，构筑起庞大的芯片帝国。我们将分析竞争对手为何始终无法复制这种垂直整合，梳理苹果在 Fab 18 及先进封装设施中的制造版图，并评估随着英特尔18A 与三星（Samsung）重新入局成为可行的替代方案，双方所面临的战略抉择。我们还将探讨双方合作关系的下一阶段。先进制程领域的玩家，如今已不再只有苹果。上述所有观点均由我们独家[[[LINK_1|晶圆代工行业模型]]]与[[[LINK_2|苹果晶圆需求模型]]]的具体数据提供量化支撑。

applied_terms: Ensure 'Anchor Tenant' is translated consistently as a foundational business concept (e.g., 锚定客户 or 基石客户).
Differentiate 'bleeding-edge' (often implying higher risk/cost, e.g., 尖端/流血前沿产能) from standard 'leading-edge' (先进制程).
Maintain a highly analytical, authoritative tone suitable for hardcore tech and finance readers.

understanding.role_in_article: Establishes the core thesis of the article, framing the Apple-TSMC relationship as a historically foundational alliance that is now being structurally disrupted by NVIDIA and the AI boom.
understanding.relation_to_previous: None. This is the opening section.
understanding.relation_to_next: Transitions from a high-level overview of the shifting power dynamics to specific, quantifiable financial evidence of Apple's historical impact on TSMC in the 'Anchor Tenant' section.
key_points: The foundry landscape is experiencing a power shift from mobile-driven (Apple) to AI-driven (NVIDIA) demand.
Apple's silicon empire was built via deep vertical integration, 8,000+ engineers, and 15 design centers, making it highly defensible.
Alternative foundries like Intel 18A and Samsung are emerging as viable options, raising strategic questions for the future.
translation_notes: Ensure 'Anchor Tenant' is translated consistently as a foundational business concept (e.g., 锚定客户 or 基石客户).
Differentiate 'bleeding-edge' (often implying higher risk/cost, e.g., 尖端/流血前沿产能) from standard 'leading-edge' (先进制程).
Maintain a highly analytical, authoritative tone suitable for hardcore tech and finance readers.

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少空格（“英特尔18A”），且对“英特尔”和“三星”等行业极度常见的品牌进行英文括注（Intel, Samsung）属于过度加注，略微打断了阅读的流畅感。
“AI 重塑晶圆代工厂（foundry）版图下的权力动态演变”作为标题或副标题略显生硬。英文的 "as" 表伴随状态，转化为“随着……而演变”会更符合中文语感。同时，foundry 括注在此处略显多余。
